meaning: adj.(艺术)非形象的,抽象的,没有比喻用法的
examples: Physics is nonfigurative. It is based on concept, and it integrates a symbol system;
The method is fit to the teaching on the nonfigurative subject such as the zoophysiology because it is visualize and lifelikeness and understandability .
His nonfigurative pursuit toward his life during this period has intrigued many scholars who have made deep researches into it with their own thinking and emotional experience.
Mr. Twist's rendering of music, once thought to be outrageously brutal and assaulting, proceeds in eye-filling and variously articulated stages with consistently nonfigurative elements.
